name: metabolical
description: Robert Lustig's Metabolical — health and nutrition toolkit exposing how processed food acts as a slow poison, how modern medicine fails chronic disease, and how the two precepts of 'protect the liver, feed the gut' can reverse the metabolic crisis
useCase: Understand how processed food causes chronic disease, learn the two precepts for real food, navigate the food industry's manipulation, and reverse metabolic dysfunction with diet
knownRisks: Lustig's strong claims about the food industry and medical establishment are not universally accepted
Some of his biochemical arguments (e.g., fructose as a toxin) are more nuanced than the book suggests
Individual dietary needs vary — the two precepts are a starting point, not a complete protocol
